CKV_AWS_20.title: Ensure S3 bucket public access block is enabled
CKV_AWS_20.description: Checks that the S3 Public Access Block configuration is applied to the bucket.
CKV_AWS_20.impact: S3 buckets without public access block policies can be exposed through misconfigured ACLs or bucket policies, leading to unauthorized data disclosure or modification.
remediation_steps: Create an aws_s3_bucket_public_access_block resource in your Terraform configuration.
Associate the public access block resource with the S3 bucket via the bucket ID.
Set block_public_acls, block_public_policy, ignore_public_acls, and restrict_public_buckets to true.
CKV_AWS_20.example:
resource "aws_s3_bucket_public_access_block" "block_public" {
  bucket = aws_s3_bucket.my_bucket.id

  block_public_acls       = true
  block_public_policy     = true
  ignore_public_acls      = true
  restrict_public_buckets = true
}

CKV_AWS_21.title: Ensure S3 bucket has versioning enabled
CKV_AWS_21.description: Checks that versioning is enabled on S3 buckets.
CKV_AWS_21.impact: Without versioning, objects can be permanently deleted or overwritten accidentally, making recovery from data corruption or malicious destruction difficult.
remediation_steps: Add a versioning block or resource to the S3 bucket configuration.
Set the versioning status to Enabled.

CKV_AWS_53.title: Ensure S3 bucket has public policies blocked
CKV_AWS_53.description: Checks if the S3 bucket restricts bucket policies from allowing public access.
CKV_AWS_53.impact: Allowing public policies can expose private assets inside the S3 bucket to anonymous internet users.
remediation_steps: Ensure block_public_policy is set to true on the public access block resource.
Audit bucket policies to remove statements containing Principal: * or wildcard permissions.
